verbGlossary.LOCATED AT: {source} is geographically situated at a project-level location where the work takes place
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source}is tagged to a specific floor or level in {target} breakdown structure for tracking and sequencing
verbGlossary.ZONED TO: {target} is allocated to a specific zone or wing within a floor, enabling zone-based scheduling and crew flow
verbGlossary.PERFORMED IN: Physical work described by {target} occurs inside a specific space, area, or level
verbGlossary.AGGREGATES ACROSS: Summary {target} or WBS element rolls up detail activities spanning multiple locations into a single line
verbGlossary.GATES RELEASE OF: {source}must be achieved before {target} is released for the next wave of work or occupancy
verbGlossary.TAGS BY: Activity code values encode {target} assignment (floor, zone, building) on each schedule activity for filtering
verbGlossary.SEQUENCES ACROSS: {source}define the floor-to-floor or zone-to-zone execution order of activities in location-based scheduling
verbGlossary.OFFSETS BETWEEN: {source}creates a mandatory time gap between the same {target} performed in successive locations (e.g., cure time between floors)
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINED BY ACCESS TO: {target} start is restricted until a site location such as a right-of-way or laydown area becomes accessible
verbGlossary.FORECASTS COMPLETION OF: Calculated early/late dates project when work at a specific location will be finished based on current logic
verbGlossary.THREADS THROUGH: {target} runs through activities at specific locations, making those locations schedule-critical
verbGlossary.BUFFERS WORK IN: Float available on activities at {target} provides time buffer before delays at that location affect the project end date
verbGlossary.PLANS WORK ON: Look-ahead {target} details the near-term activities planned for a specific floor, zone, or room
verbGlossary.REPORTS PROGRESS ON: {source} captures percent complete and remaining duration for activities at a specific location
verbGlossary.BASELINES SEQUENCE OF: {target} locks the planned order in which locations (buildings, floors) will be constructed
verbGlossary.BASELINES DATES FOR: {source}approved start and finish targets for work at a specific floor or level
verbGlossary.RESEQUENCES WORK ON: Recovery {target} changes the order or overlap of work at specific locations to regain lost time
verbGlossary.RECORDS ACTUAL DATES FOR: {source}schedule captures the true start and finish dates of work completed at each {target}
verbGlossary.MOBILIZED TO: {source}is moved to a specific floor or zone to begin {target}d work at that location
verbGlossary.STAGED AT: {source}is pre-positioned at a laydown or staging area before deployment to the active work location
verbGlossary.LEVELS ACROSS: {source}leveling adjusts {target} timing so that crews and equipment are balanced across locations without overloading any single area
verbGlossary.ALLOCATES COST TO: Cost loading distributes budgeted dollars to activities at specific locations for location-based cash flow forecasting
verbGlossary.LOCATES DELAY AT: {source}identifies which physical location experienced the delay event for claims documentation
verbGlossary.ANALYZES IMPACT ON: {source}evaluates how a delay at a specific location ripples through the overall {target}
verbGlossary.ACCELERATES WORK ON: {source}plan adds overtime, crews, or shifts to compress {target} at a specific floor or zone
verbGlossary.PACES THROUGH: {source}planning sets a fixed production cadence for crews flowing through successive zones or floors in a repeating cycle
verbGlossary.FLOWS THROUGH: Work flows continuously through a building as takt-time crews cycle from zone to zone and floor to floor
verbGlossary.PROGRESSES THROUGH: {source}maps activity progress along {target} axis (floor-by-floor or station-by-station) on a time-distance diagram
verbGlossary.PROGRESSES ALONG: Linear scheduling sequences work along a horizontal location path such as a pipeline, roadway, or right-of-way
verbGlossary.COMMITS WORK IN: {source}Planner weekly work plan assigns crew commitments to specific zones or floors for the upcoming period
verbGlossary.SIMULATES SEQUENCE OF: 4D BIM model animates the construction sequence at each {target} to visualize spatial and temporal conflicts
verbGlossary.ANIMATES ERECTION AT: 4D simulation shows structural erection progressing at grid-line intersections to verify crane reach and sequencing
verbGlossary.LINKS FIELD WORK AT: {source}-to-field linkage connects planned activities to daily logs and progress recorded at a specific location
verbGlossary.STATUSES WORK ON: Data date marks the point through which progress at a specific location has been recorded in {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURES PROGRESS ON: {source}schedule analysis computes time-based performance metrics for work completed at {target}
verbGlossary.TRACES PATH THROUGH: {source}identifies the longest or near-{target} running through activities at specific locations
verbGlossary.DEFINES BOUNDARY FOR: {source} area establishes the physical limits within which {target}d activity is permitted to operate
verbGlossary.DEFINES ZONE FOR: {source}creates the repeating spatial unit used in takt-time or Last Planner zone-based planning
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source}of all activities at {target} triggers an associated milestone or metric update in the schedule
verbGlossary.FEEDS: {source}-based completion rate provides actual progress data into {target} performance calculations
verbGlossary.VALIDATES: {source} coverage or completion metric cross-checks {target}'s reported percent plan complete
verbGlossary.CORRELATES WITH: {source} coverage rate statistically moves in tandem with overall {target} health scoring
verbGlossary.ALIGNED WITH: {source} depth matches the activity code structure used to tag activities by location in {target}
verbGlossary.ASSESSES SCHEDULE FOR: {source}check evaluates {target} quality for activities assigned to a specific building or location set
verbGlossary.SCORED BY: {source} health score is computed for activities assigned to a specific location to identify problem areas
verbGlossary.MONITORED ON: {target} erosion rate is tracked at the floor level to detect where schedule buffer is being consumed fastest
verbGlossary.MEASURED ACROSS: {source}rate is evaluated across all locations within a building to assess overall {target} reliability
verbGlossary.MEASURED IN: {source} performance metric is calculated for activities within a specific zone to measure zone-level execution
verbGlossary.DETECTED ON: {source}is identified at a specific floor where activities are executing ahead of or behind their planned logic
verbGlossary.VALIDATED FOR: {source} specification compliance is checked for activities assigned to a specific building or location grouping
verbGlossary.TRACKED ON: {source}is captured at the floor or zone level for location-specific status reporting
verbGlossary.REQUIRES CLEARANCE FROM: Fire/smoke compartment boundaries require {target}s for firestopping inspection before adjacent work proceeds
verbGlossary.REQUIRES TURNOVER OF: {source}must reach a turnover milestone in {target} before functional testing can begin in that zone
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS STAGING FOR: {source}area capacity limits the volume of equipment and material that can be staged, constraining {target} loading
verbGlossary.SUPPORTS: Temporary facility (trailer, temp power, temp water) must be operational to support {target}d activities at the site
